questions.question: Which request formula gives Claude the best result?
options: Keywords separated by commas, like in a search engine
Context + a specific task + the desired answer format
The shortest possible command — the model will figure it out
Say hello first, then ask a general question

questions.question: What is a model's "knowledge cutoff"?
options: The date your Claude subscription expires
The maximum age of files you can upload to a chat
The date after which the model doesn't know about events without web search
How long chat history is stored on the servers

questions.question: How does a Project in claude.ai differ from a regular chat?
options: A project runs on a more powerful model
A project has its own instructions and knowledge base, shared across all chats inside it
A project is a chat you can share via a link
A project has no limit on the number of messages

questions.question: What are Artifacts in claude.ai?
options: An archive of all files uploaded to chats
A log of past versions of Claude's answers
Paid add-ons to the Pro subscription
Interactive documents and mini-apps that Claude creates in a separate panel

questions.question: Which field in SKILL.md decides whether the skill fires automatically at the right moment?
options: description — Claude uses it to know when to load the skill
name — the skill is only invoked by its exact name
version — outdated skills don't run
license — without a license the skill is ignored

questions.question: What fundamentally makes a skill different from a regular prompt?
options: A skill is always written in Python, and a prompt in plain text
A skill is a reusable package of instructions and resources that loads automatically based on the task description
A skill only works in Claude Code, and a prompt only in the chat
Nothing: "skill" is just a marketing name for a long prompt

questions.question: Why does a repository need a CLAUDE.md file at its root?
options: It's the license agreement for using Claude Code
It's a template for generating the project README
It's the project's memory: commands, rules, and conventions that Claude Code reads every session
It's a log of every change the agent has made

questions.question: What does the /init command do in Claude Code?
options: Creates a new git repository
Studies the codebase and creates a CLAUDE.md file describing it
Resets the current session's context
Installs the project's dependencies

questions.question: What is a PreToolUse hook in Claude Code?
options: A hotkey for quickly invoking a tool
A plugin that adds new tools to the agent
A Slack notification before a session starts
A command that runs automatically before a tool call and can block it

questions.question: The main benefit of subagents in Claude Code is to…
options: delegate subtasks with a separate context and do work in parallel
speed up the model by caching its answers
run Claude Code without an internet connection
automatically fix all compilation errors

questions.question: Which three types of capabilities does an MCP server provide to the client?
options: Models, Chats, and Files
Tools, Resources, and Prompts
Commands, Agents, and Hooks
Skills, Plugins, and Connectors

questions.question: What can a Claude Code plugin be made of?
options: A single skill only
Only terminal appearance settings
Commands, agents, hooks, skills, and MCP servers in one package
A set of ready-made prompts for claude.ai chat

questions.question: How does the agentic loop work with tool use in the Messages API?
options: The model runs the tools itself on Anthropic's servers and sends back a finished result
Tools are only available through the Agent SDK; the Messages API doesn't have them
The client runs all the tools in advance and attaches the results to the first request
The model returns a tool_use block, your code runs the tool and sends a tool_result back

questions.question: What does prompt caching give you in the Claude API?
options: The model remembers the user across different applications
Answers become deterministic — identical on every request
A significant discount and speedup for the repeated part of the prompt that has already been processed
Free requests within the first thousand tokens

questions.question: What is prompt injection?
options: Malicious instructions hidden in data (an email, a page, a file) that the agent reads and may execute
A way to speed up the answer by inserting the prompt into the system message
A network error that causes the prompt to be sent twice
A legitimate prompt-engineering technique for bypassing limits

questions.question: How should you handle secrets (API keys, passwords) when working with AI agents?
options: Paste them straight into the prompt — the conversation is private anyway
Keep them in code comments so the agent can see them
Store them in environment variables or a secret vault and never pass them into prompts
It's enough to ask the agent to "forget" the key after using it
